meta.note: Structured evidence only — no raw player/session rows. The AI Investigator sees this and nothing else.

packets.title: New player bad table mix

top_evidence.detail: Predation pressure 40/45 — skill-weighted aggressor load 2.0 against 0 recreational/new seat(s).
Fragility 22/25 — 3/6 seats, paid-seat-time trend 'flat'. Short-handed concentrates exposure.
No observed recreational truncation yet (composition-led snapshot).
ΔHealth +3.6 — marginal change to table composition health if seated (P_bleed held fixed).
new player at a 'healthy' table (Health 94). New/recreational players are the cohort this protects — risk low.
Account registered 4 day(s) ago (<= 14); treat as a new, vulnerable player.

counter_evidence.detail: Counter-evidence was assessed; no material exculpatory signal was found in the available evidence. Absence of counter-evidence is not corroboration — weigh the primary signals against the uncertainties.

uncertainties: This is an elevated-for-review signal, not a determination of wrongdoing. A human operator decides; no automatic action is taken.
All signals are simulated fields on synthetic data — not real device telemetry, KYC, location, or real-time gameplay/RTA.
Health and seating scores are composition-based projections of table state, not realized outcomes — an unhealthy mix is a risk, not proof of harm.

provenance.prd_ref: PRD §6 New Player Case / §7 Eval A

packets.title: Strong grinder health concern not integrity

top_evidence.detail: High-volume grind (285,000 lifetime hands) with strong postflop aggression (AF 2.80); a table-health consideration, explicitly NOT an integrity flag.

counter_evidence.detail: No integrity signals (no device link, soft-play, or correlated win/loss). The pattern is explained by skill / legitimate play, not coordination — a health lens, not an integrity accusation.

uncertainties: This is an elevated-for-review signal, not a determination of wrongdoing. A human operator decides; no automatic action is taken.
All signals are simulated fields on synthetic data — not real device telemetry, KYC, location, or real-time gameplay/RTA.
Health and seating scores are composition-based projections of table state, not realized outcomes — an unhealthy mix is a risk, not proof of harm.

packets.title: True coordinated cluster integrity review

top_evidence.detail: Shared device link (DG-001) ties cluster accounts to the same device group.
Pairwise session-timing correlation up to 0.88 (>= 0.8) across members.
Within-cluster soft-play: soft_play_delta down to -0.82 (<= -0.6) — value given away in member-vs-member hands.
Outsider-pressure signal present (coordinated aggression toward non-members); corroborating context, not a primary family.
Elevated casual-player exit impact at affected tables.

counter_evidence.detail: Counter-evidence was assessed; no material exculpatory signal was found in the available evidence. Absence of counter-evidence is not corroboration — weigh the primary signals against the uncertainties.

uncertainties: This is an elevated-for-review signal, not a determination of wrongdoing. A human operator decides; no automatic action is taken.
All signals are simulated fields on synthetic data — not real device telemetry, KYC, location, or real-time gameplay/RTA.
Convergent signals are consistent with coordination but can also reflect benign shared context (household, friends, similar schedules); none is individually conclusive.

Primary integrity review case. Three convergent signals: device link (A/B), timing correlation (C to both), repeated co-seating, within-cluster soft_play_delta. Counter-evidence: no individual signal alone proves collusion — all three must converge.

provenance.prd_ref: PRD §6 Coordinated Cluster Case / §7 Eval C
provenance.note: Structured evidence only — assembled from frozen P3 outputs; no raw rows.

packets.title: Regular overlap not over escalated

counter_evidence.detail: High co-seating (rate 0.50) but no device link, no soft-play, no correlated win/loss — explained by shared stake/time preference, not coordination.

uncertainties: This is an elevated-for-review signal, not a determination of wrongdoing. A human operator decides; no automatic action is taken.
All signals are simulated fields on synthetic data — not real device telemetry, KYC, location, or real-time gameplay/RTA.
Convergent signals are consistent with coordination but can also reflect benign shared context (household, friends, similar schedules); none is individually conclusive.
TRAP for over-eager classifiers. High overlap without collusion signals is expected for players who share a preferred session window.
Flagged in the data as a false-positive trap: the convergent-looking overlap has a benign explanation (see counter-evidence).

provenance.note: Structured evidence only — assembled from frozen P3 outputs; no raw rows.

packets.title: Shared device household false positive

top_evidence.detail: Shared device (DG-002) — expected for a household; not incriminating on its own.

counter_evidence.detail: Divergent session profiles and low co-seating (rate 0.09); no soft-play, no correlated profitability. Shared-device overlap is explained by household, not collusion.

uncertainties: This is an elevated-for-review signal, not a determination of wrongdoing. A human operator decides; no automatic action is taken.
All signals are simulated fields on synthetic data — not real device telemetry, KYC, location, or real-time gameplay/RTA.
Convergent signals are consistent with coordination but can also reflect benign shared context (household, friends, similar schedules); none is individually conclusive.

FALSE POSITIVE TRAP. Same mocked device, but radically different session profiles — H1 plays evenings (~145 min), H2 plays mornings (~110 min), different vpip/pfr. Low co-seating rate (2 of 22). No soft-play, no correlated profit. Must produce monitor-only outcome, NOT escalation or suppression.
Flagged in the data as a false-positive trap: the convergent-looking overlap has a benign explanation (see counter-evidence).

provenance.prd_ref: PRD §6 Shared-Device False Positive / §7 Eval E

packets.title: Promo abuse not collusion

top_evidence.detail: 21 promo redemptions in 30d with short ~32-min sessions — promo abuse, a health/economics concern, not collusion.

A promo-redemption pattern indicates bonus optimization, which is a policy/health matter and distinct from collusion or cheating.
